reasoning: Since the Oct 16 market baseline of 55%, tensions have significantly escalated. On October 24, Defense Secretary Hegseth ordered the USS Gerald R. The explicit U.S. admission of planning land strikes (which instantly triggers a 'Yes' per the resolution criteria) coupled with the heightened risk of an accidental surface-to-air missile exchange during drills makes a military engagement highly probable. Though an October 31 deadline provides a very narrow window, sufficient U.S. assets (like bombers and the 8 warships already stationed) are already in place, and political incentives align with pre-election action. If the question's 'December 31' title timeline applies, the arrival of the carrier strike group in November makes a ground strike or direct clash even more likely.
